hem_core_version.description: The core version the input is written for. If there is a mismatch a warning will be printed

PartGcompliance.description: Indication for whether Part G has been complied with

BuildingLength.description: The maximum horizontal distance (in metres) across the building footprint

BuildingWidth.description: The maximum horizontal distance (in metres) perpendicular to the building length

NumberOfWetRooms.description: A wet room is any room used for domestic activities (such as cooking, clothes washing and bathing) that produce significant amounts of airborne moisture, e.g. a kitchen, utility room or bathroom. For the purposes of Part F of the Building Regulations, sanitary accommodation is also regarded as a wet room.

NumberOfHotTappedRooms.description: A tapped room is any room with a hot water tapping point (e.g sink, bath or shower) with hot water from the central system, not including instantaneous hot water that's fed from the cold water pipework, for example a room with just an electric shower and/or electric water heater over the sink.

NumberOfUtilityRooms.description: A utility room is any that contains a sink or other feature or equipment that may reasonably be expected to produce significant quantities of water vapour

NumberOfBathrooms.description: A bathroom is any room that contains a bath or shower

NumberOfSanitaryAccommodations.description: Any space containing one or more flush toilets or urinals but not a bath or shower. Multiple cubicles counts as one space as long as there is free circulation of air throughout

NumberOfHabitableRooms.description: A habitable room is any that is not used solely as a kitchen, bathroom, utility, cellar or sanitary accommodation

KitchenExtractorHoodExternal.description: Does the kitchen have a cooker extractor hood which extracts to the outside of the building?

start360.description: Starting angle of the shading segment (clockwise). (unit: °)

end360.description: The end angle of the shading segment (clockwise). (unit: °)

height.description: Height of the shading object. (Unit: m)

distance.description: Distance of the shading object from the building. (Unit: m)

header tank.description: The content of this object can be blank, as the FHS wrapper will generate the HEM ColdWaterSource.

mains water.description: The content of this object can be blank, as the FHS wrapper will generate the HEM ColdWaterSource.

peak_power.description: Peak power; represents the electrical power of a photovoltaic system with a given area for a solar irradiance of 1 kW/m² on this surface (at 25 degrees) (unit: kW)

pitch.description: The tilt angle (inclination) of the PV panel from horizontal, measured upwards facing, 0 to 90 (unit: ˚)

orientation360.description: The orientation angle of the inclined surface, expressed as the geographical azimuth angle of the horizontal projection of the inclined surface normal, 0 to 360 (unit: ˚)

base_height.description: The distance between the ground and the lowest edge of the PV array (unit: m)

height.description: Height of the PV array (unit: m)

width.description: Width of the PV panel (unit: m)

EnergySupply.description: References a key (e.g., 'mains elec', 'mains gas') in $.EnergySupply

inverter_peak_power_dc.description: Peak power; represents the peak electrical DC power input to the inverter (unit: kW)

inverter_peak_power_ac.description: Peak power; represents the peak electrical AC power output from the inverter (unit: kW)

inverter_is_inside.description: Whether the inverter is considered inside the building

efficiency.description: Thermal efficiency of the point-of-use water heater (dimensionless, 0-1)

EnergySupply.description: References a key (e.g., 'mains elec', 'mains gas') in $.EnergySupply

product_reference.description: A unique reference to a product held within the HEM database (PCDB)
product_reference.title: Reference to the product in the HEM database

rejected_energy_1.description: Rejected energy factor 1 for combi boiler efficiency calculations (unit: kWh)
rejected_energy_1.title: Rejected Energy 1

rejected_factor_3.description: Rejected energy factor 3 for combi boiler efficiency calculations (dimensionless)
rejected_factor_3.title: Rejected Factor 3

separate_DHW_tests.description: Type of separate domestic hot water test performed on the combi boiler (M&L, M&S, M_only, or No_additional_tests)

storage_loss_factor_1.description: Storage loss factor 1 for combi boiler efficiency calculations (unit: kWh/day)
storage_loss_factor_1.title: Storage Loss Factor 1

storage_loss_factor_2.description: Storage loss factor 2 for combi boiler efficiency calculations (dimensionless)
storage_loss_factor_2.title: Storage Loss Factor 2

is_heat_network.description: Is this HeatSourceWet a heat network? Put false if it is a HeatPump that is supplied by a HeatNetwork. In that case you will instead describe the heat network source under EnergySupply_heat_network not the EnergySupply of the pump

heat_network_type.description: What type of heat network is present?

required: Emissions Factor kgCO2e/kWh including out-of-scope emissions

heat_network_reference.description: A unique (product) reference to a heat network held within the HEM database (PCDB)
heat_network_reference.title: Reference to the heat network in the HEM database

sub_heat_network_name.description: The name of a sub heat network that is part of the heat network referenced by heat_network_reference
sub_heat_network_name.title: Name of the sub heat network

EnergySupply.description: References a key (e.g., 'mains elec', 'mains gas') in $.EnergySupply

product_reference.description: A unique reference to a product held within the HEM database (PCDB)
product_reference.title: Reference to the product in the HEM database

pump_fixed_flow_rate.description: Flow rate of the buffer tank - emitters loop (unit: l/min)

pump_power_at_flow_rate.description: Pump power of the buffer tank - emitters loop (unit: kW)

volume.description: Volume of the buffer tank (unit: litre)

BufferTank.description: Optional buffer tank configuration for the heat pump system

boiler_product_reference.description: A unique reference to a boiler product held within the HEM database (PCDB), if there is a need for a reference to a boiler product to be passed, and the heat pump is not packaged with a boiler.
boiler_product_reference.title: Reference to the product in the HEM database

boiler.description: A container for a boiler's cost_schedule_hybrid, if this is available. This field will be merged onto the data for any boiler packaged with the indicated heat pump product.

source_type.description: Type of heat source for the heat pump

sink_type.description: Type of heat sink for the heat pump

backup_ctrl_type.description: Type of backup control for the heat pump system

modulating_control.description: Whether the heat pump uses modulating control

min_modulation_rate_20.description: Minimum modulation rate at 20°C flow temperature (dimensionless, 0-1)

min_modulation_rate_35.description: Minimum modulation rate at 35°C flow temperature (dimensionless, 0-1)

min_modulation_rate_55.description: Minimum modulation rate at 55°C flow temperature (dimensionless, 0-1)

temp_lower_operating_limit.description: Lower temperature limit for heat pump operation (unit: ˚C)

min_temp_diff_flow_return_for_hp_to_oper.description: Minimum temperature difference between flow and return for heat pump operation (unit: K)

var_flow_temp_ctrl_during_test.description: Whether variable flow temperature control was used during testing

power_heating_circ_pump.description: Power consumption of heating circuit pump (unit: kW)

power_source_circ_pump.description: Power consumption of source circuit pump (unit: kW)

power_standby.description: Power consumption in standby mode (unit: kW)

power_crankcase_heater.description: Power consumption of crankcase heater (unit: kW)

power_off.description: Power consumption when heat pump is off (unit: kW)

pump_power_at_flow_rate.description: Pump power of the buffer tank - emitters loop (unit: kW)

power_heating_warm_air_fan.description: Power consumption of warm air fan (unit: kW)

time_constant_onoff_operation.description: Time constant for on/off operation (unit: seconds)

efficiency_part_load.description: Boiler net efficiency at part load (dimensionless, 0-1.12). Net efficiencies may exceed 1 in test data.
efficiency_part_load.title: Efficiency Part Load

psi_wall_floor_junc.description: Linear thermal transmittance of the junction between the floor and the walls (unit: W/m.K)

thermal_resistance_floor_construction.description: Total thermal resistance of all layers in the floor construction, excluding surface resistances (unit: m².K/W)

edge_thermal_resistance.description: Thermal resistance of floor edge insulation (unit: m²K/W)

width.description: Width of horizontal edge insulation (unit: m)

depth.description: Depth of vertical edge insulation (unit: m)

height_upper_surface.description: Height of the floor upper surface (unit: m) - average value is used if h varies

thermal_transm_walls.description: Thermal transmittance of walls above ground in accordance with ISO 6946, i.e. including surface resistances (unit: W/m².K)

area_per_perimeter_vent.description: Area of ventilation openings per perimeter (unit: m²/m)

thermal_resist_insul.description: Thermal resistance of insulation on base of underfloor space, excluding surface resistances (unit: m².K/W)

depth_basement_floor.description: Depth of basement floor below ground level (unit: m)

thermal_resist_walls_base.description: Thermal resistance of walls of the basement, excluding surface resistances (unit: m².K/W)

thermal_transm_envi_base.description: Thermal transmittance of floor above basement in accordance with ISO 6946, i.e. including surface resistances (unit: W/m².K)

thermal_transm_walls.description: Thermal transmittance of walls above ground in accordance with ISO 6946, i.e. including surface resistances (unit: W/m².K)

height_basement_walls.description: Height of the basement walls above ground level (unit: m)

pitch.description: Tilt angle of the surface from horizontal, between 0 and 180, where 0 means the external surface is facing up, 90 means the external surface is vertical and 180 means the external surface is facing down (unit: ˚)

base_height.description: The distance between the ground and the lowest edge of the element (unit: m)

height.description: Height of the building element (m), must be positive

width.description: Width of the building element (m), must be positive

area.description: Area of the building element (m²), must be positive

orientation360.description: Degrees from north. Required for any element with a non-flat (0 or 180) pitch

then.description:
Party wall element for all party wall types, with specific handling for cavity air movement heat loss.

For cavity party walls, this accounts for non-negligible heat loss due to air movement
within the cavity, as identified by research. The thermal resistance of the
unconditioned space (cavity) is derived from the party wall cavity type and lining type.
Five cavity types are supported:
solid, unfilled_unsealed, unfilled_sealed, filled_sealed, and defined_resistance.
Two lining types are supported:
wet_plaster, and dry_lined

area.description: Area of the building element (m²), must be positive
area.title: Area

pitch.description: Tilt angle of the surface from horizontal, between 60 and 120 degrees (wall range), where 90 means vertical (unit: °)
pitch.title: Pitch

party_wall_cavity_type.description: Type of party wall cavity construction affecting heat loss through air movement

party_wall_lining_type.description: Type of party wall lining. Required only when party_wall_cavity_type is unfilled_unsealed, unfilled_sealed, or filled_unsealed

thermal_resistance_cavity.description: Effective thermal resistance of the party wall cavity (unit: m².K/W). Required only when party_wall_cavity_type is 'defined_resistance'. For other cavity types, this is calculated automatically.
thermal_resistance_cavity.title: Thermal Resistance Cavity

pitch.description: Tilt angle of the surface from horizontal, between 0 and 180, where 0 means the external surface is facing up, 90 means the external surface is vertical and 180 means the external surface is facing down (unit: ˚)

frame_area_fraction.description: The frame area fraction of window, ratio of the projected frame area to the overall projected area of the glazed element of the window

g_value.description: Total solar energy transmittance of the transparent part of the window

free_area_height.description: Height of the openable area, corrected for obstruction due to the window frame of the openable section (unit: m)

mid_height.description: Height of the mid-point of the window, relative to the base of the ventilation zone (unit: m)

max_window_open_area.description: Openable area of the window ignoring the obstructing effect of the frame of the openable part

delta_r.description: Additional thermal resistance provided by a window treatment (unit: m²K/W)

trans_red.description: Dimensionless factor describing the reduction in the amount of transmitted radiation due to a window treatment

base_height.description: The distance between the ground and the lowest edge of the element (unit: m)

height.description: Height of the building element (m), must be positive

width.description: Width of the building element (m), must be positive

orientation360.description: Degrees from north. Required for any element with a non-flat (0 or 180) pitch

BuildingElement.description: Dictionary of building elements present in the zone (e.g. walls, floors, windows, etc.).

linear_thermal_transmittance.description: Linear thermal transmittance of the thermal bridge. (Unit: W/m.K)

length.description: Length of the thermal bridge over which the linear thermal transmittance applies. (Unit: m)

heat_transfer_coeff.description: Heat transfer coefficient of the thermal bridge. (Unit: W/K)

ThermalBridging.description: Overall heat transfer coefficient of the thermal bridge (in W/K), or dictionary of linear thermal transmittance details of the thermal bridges in the zone.

capacity.description: The maximum capacity of the battery (unit: kWh)

charge_discharge_efficiency_round_trip.description: Charge/discharge round trip efficiency of battery system (greater than 0, up to 1)

minimum_charge_rate_one_way_trip.description: The minimum charge rate one way trip the battery allows (unit: kW)

maximum_charge_rate_one_way_trip.description: The maximum charge rate one way trip the battery allows (unit: kW)

maximum_discharge_rate_one_way_trip.description: The maximum discharge rate one way trip the battery allows (unit: kW)

enum: I: Mass concentrated at internal side
E: Mass concentrated at external side
IE: Mass divided over internal and external side
MassDistributionClass.title: MassDistributionClass

cost_schedule_boiler.description: Cost data for the fuel used by the hybrid's boiler (can be any units, typically p/kWh)

cost_schedule_hp.description: Cost data for the fuel used by the hybrid's heat pump (can be any units, typically p/kWh)

cost_schedule_start_day.description: Day on which the cost data series begins
cost_schedule_start_day.title: Cost Schedule Start Day

cost_schedule_time_series_step.description: Time step of the cost data series
cost_schedule_time_series_step.title: Cost Schedule Time Series Step

SFP.description: Specific fan power, assumed inclusive of any in use factors unless SFP_in_use_factor also provided (unit: W/l/s)

ScheduleForDouble.description:
A dictionary of schedule entries where:
- Keys are user-defined names (e.g., "main", "week", "weekday", "weekend")
- Values are lists of ScheduleEntryForDouble
- The "main" entry is required
ScheduleForDouble.title: ScheduleForDouble

ScheduleEntryForDouble.description:
A schedule entry that can be a direct value, repeating pattern, or reference.

Examples:
    # Direct float value (temperature setpoint)
    21.5

    # Repeating pattern (21.5°C for 8 hours, 18°C for 16 hours)
    {"repeat": 24, "value": 21.5}

    # Reference to another schedule
    "weekday_temp_schedule"

    # Complex repeating pattern with reference
    {"repeat": 7, "value": "daily_temp_pattern"}

    # Power level schedule
    2.5  # 2.5 kW constant power
ScheduleEntryForDouble.title: ScheduleEntryForDouble

repeat.description: Number of times to repeat the value
repeat.title: Repeat

value.description: Value to repeat or schedule reference

ScheduleRepeaterForDouble.title: ScheduleRepeaterForDouble

PartyWallCavityType.description: Types of party wall cavity configurations
PartyWallCavityType.title: PartyWallCavityType

product_reference.description: A unique reference to a product held within the HEM database (PCDB)
